claim: Planning is the single most powerful pattern for scaling AI coding impact with powerful reasoning models
actionable: Before writing code with AI, create detailed spec documents with implementation details and step-by-step tasks for the AI to follow
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Spec-prompt-driven workflows enable multiplying engineering effort by delegating to multiple AI coding tools in parallel
actionable: Write a detailed spec once, then execute it simultaneously with multiple AI tools (Devin, Cursor, Aider) in parallel 'plan-wait-review' loops
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Single-file agents using UV inline dependencies provide a sustainable pattern for scalable AI-generated code
actionable: Use UV inline script dependencies to package agents as single executable files with embedded dependencies, avoiding dependency hell
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Verification commands embedded in specs allow agents to self-validate their work by running tests and reporting results
actionable: Add a 'verify' step to your spec that runs specific commands to validate the agent's implementation, creating a closed feedback loop
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Devon performed a 100-line spec to 600-line production-quality change with fewer issues than cursor's composer in agent mode
actionable: Use Devin for large-scale feature implementation when you have detailed specs, as it handles agent orchestration and self-verification better than cursor composer currently
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Modeling is only 30% of AI coding effort; the remaining 70% is pipelines, monitoring, scaling, and infrastructure
actionable: Allocate your team capacity accordingly: spend minimal time optimizing model outputs and maximum time building robust deployment pipelines and monitoring
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Minimal PRs (under 1000 lines) are essential for effective agent-based code review with tools like Greptile
actionable: When splitting large features into PRs for AI review, aim for 300-800 lines per PR so agents can catch edge cases and security issues
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Pack patterns is an experimental lightweight AI coding tool that embeds plan-based patterns for spec-driven development
actionable: Use pack patterns CLI (available to Principled AI Coding members) to scaffold spec-driven workflows with built-in patterns for director-mode agent execution
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: The evaluator command pattern enables closed-loop code generation where failing tests feed back to the agent for auto-remediation
actionable: Define 'evaluator_command' in specs that run tests and pipe output back to the agent up to 5 iterations until success or max turns reached
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: If not using AI coding tools in 2025, engineers will not maintain an upward trajectory into 2026
actionable: Adopt at least one modern AI coding tool (Cursor, Devin, Claude Code, Aider) into your weekly workflow immediately
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern

claim: Composable architecture patterns with service layer abstractions enable agents to understand and modify large codebases reliably
actionable: Structure your code as independent, well-documented services with clear interfaces so agents (and humans) can find and modify code without breaking other parts
source_title: Scale your AI Coding IMPACT with Devin, Cursor, Aider and this ONE Pattern
